element_introduction_order.first_appears: Ultimate Mortal Kombat 3 (new to UMK3 vs MK3)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Early matches teach jump-initiated combo mechanics

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Original Mortal Kombat series, present in UMK3
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Victory condition execution after defeating opponents

element_introduction_order.first_appears: UMK3 onwards, notably Scorpion's Lair lava uppercut
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Stage-specific environmental hazards become finishing opportunities

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mortal Kombat Trilogy (1996), incorporated into SNES UMK3
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Advanced technique requiring extended combo execution

element_introduction_order.first_appears: UMK3 (new feature; last seen in original MK1)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Endurance-style matches with up to three human players per round
Extended single-player progression through multiple opponent brackets
Highest difficulty tier unlocked after standard progression

element_introduction_order.element: Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures (prize selection system)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Unlocked after completing main game or Tournament mode; offers cutscenes and bonus fights

element_introduction_order.first_appears: MK series tradition, selectable in UMK3
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional meta-layer allowing stage access without progression

typical_relief_triggers: Access to Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures prize screen
typical_tension_triggers: AI opponent cornering player (enables repeated punches without AI stagger)
Improved computer AI patterns (improved from MK3)
Back-and-forth walking range trigger (AI mimicry deadlock)
Jump kick vulnerability window during backflip (AI projectile response)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade fighting game rhythm: round-based tension cycles with character-select intervals, escalating difficulty curve through ladder progression; AI exploitability creates variable tension based on player skill mastery

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Stage backgrounds feature destructible/hazard elements; Scorpion's Lair enables uppercut transitions to Kahn's Kave (vertical stage layering). Noob's Dorfen and secret stages accessible via Kombat Kode without tournament progression.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Kombat Kodes unlock stages and alternate content without standard progression; hidden sound test accessible via button combo (Left, Down, Y, Y); Tournament mode accessed via title screen hold (L+R)+Start

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial arcade ladder introduces returning characters (Reptile, Kitana, Jade, Scorpion) with standard AI patterns; new chain combo mechanics (vertical jump-start) taught organically through progression
difficulty_curve.mid_game: AI improvements from MK3 provide moderate challenge; exploitable AI flaws emerge (walking mimicry deadlock, projectile prediction during backflip, corner stagger failure) that skilled players leverage
difficulty_curve.late_game: Master difficulty setting increases opponent threat; Tournament mode brackets extend difficulty through multiple successive fights; Shao Kahn final encounter represents progression capstone
notable_spikes: Transition to Master difficulty (explicit difficulty tier jump)
Chameleon hidden character (incomplete, uses Rain lifebar + Scorpion moveset, lacks special moves)

notable_level_patterns.description: UMK3 introduces new backgrounds with environmental finishing mechanics (e.g., Scorpion's Lair lava uppercut Stage Fatality). Jade's Desert, River Kombat, Kahn's Kave, Blue Portal backgrounds feature visual storytelling (Cyrax stuck in sand reference to MK3 ending). Multi-layered stages (Scorpion's Lair/Kahn's Kave vertical transition) allow spatial progression during rounds.

SNES version introduces three documented AI flaws creating exploitability: (1) walking range mimicry deadlock prevents opponent attack, (2) corner stagger failure allows unpunished punch sequences, (3) backflip jump-kick triggers guaranteed projectile response. These create variable difficulty spikes dependent on player awareness of exploit mechanics.

Tournament mode and arcade ladder gate stage access sequentially (UMK3 backgrounds cycle twice before MK3 original stages appear). Alternate access via Kombat Kode system bypasses progression (Noob's Dorfen, secret cave, abandoned river selectable by password). This dual-path structure allows speedrunning or comprehensive stage experience.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bank Stage cycle placeholder (replaced by Jade's Desert)

notable_level_patterns.description: SNES cartridge memory constraints removed Sheeva and left Chameleon incomplete (hidden, no special moves/Fatalities). Only four returning character biographies included (Kitana, Jade, Scorpion, Reptile); all MK3 character biographies removed. This creates asymmetrical narrative progression vs. arcade version.

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Ultimate Mortal Kombat 3 (SNES)
